# AuroraPath

> Real-time, carbon-optimized aurora borealis visibility dashboard. Built for Earth Day 2026.

AuroraPath combines live NOAA space weather data with Google Gemini AI to help nature enthusiasts
find the northern lights while minimizing their carbon footprint.

## What AuroraPath Does

- Tracks real-time aurora activity using NOAA SWPC data (G-scale, Kp index, solar wind speed)
- Computes an Aurora Visibility Score (AVS, 0–100) based on live geomagnetic conditions
- Displays an interactive map of aurora visibility latitude bands that update every 30 seconds
- Generates AI-powered "Green Path" recommendations: sustainable viewing routes with CO₂ savings,
  public transit options, carpooling, walking paths, and dark-sky ratings

## Aurora Visibility Score (AVS)

The AVS is a deterministic physics model — not an AI estimate:

```
AVS = (G-Scale / 5 × 65) + (max(solarWindSpeed − 300, 0) / 500 × 25) + forecastBonus
```

| Score  | Level    | Meaning                              |
|--------|----------|--------------------------------------|
| 80–100 | Excellent | Visible at mid-latitudes (≥45°N)    |
| 60–79  | High      | Strong activity at high latitudes    |
| 35–59  | Moderate  | Visible at polar regions (≥60°N)    |
| 10–34  | Low       | Far northern regions only            |
| 0–9    | None      | Quiet geomagnetic conditions         |

## Green Path Recommendations

The Gemini AI agent generates 1–3 location recommendations per request, each containing:
- Viewing spot name and coordinates
- Distance and estimated travel time from user location
- Recommended transport mode (transit, carpool, walk, bike)
- CO₂ savings vs. solo driving (in kg)
- Dark-sky rating (1–5 Bortle scale approximation)
- Best viewing window based on current aurora forecast

Geographic safety: recommendations are constrained to within ~300 km of the user's location
and validated server-side with haversine distance checks.

## Data Sources

- NOAA Space Weather Prediction Center — https://www.swpc.noaa.gov/
- Nominatim geocoding (OpenStreetMap) — reverse geocoding via server-side proxy
- OpenStreetMap tile rendering

## Technology Stack

- Next.js 14 / React 18 / TypeScript
- Google Gemini AI (Flash) for Green Path generation
- Auth0 for user authentication and machine-to-machine API identity
- Upstash Redis for rate limiting and quota tracking
- Leaflet.js for interactive mapping

## Earth Day Mission

AuroraPath promotes low-carbon travel to aurora viewing sites. Each Green Path recommendation
calculates the CO₂ saved by choosing public transit, carpooling, or active transport over
solo private car use — making aurora chasing an act of environmental stewardship.
